hard.next_step: 6/29 - No Material Update. 6/22 - Following up with Amresh to receive updated evaluation timeline, No response yet 6/1 - No material update 5/13 - Amresh is back in office. Will follow up with some available times to meet week of 5/18 4/28 - Coordinating TIme with Amresh to schedule a demo with additional stakeholders. Amresh is out of office till 5/8
hard.ais_why:
• Champion Amresh Munshi engaged the August 19 2025 demo squarely on autonomous AI. The call confirmed strong interest in autonomous sourcing and AI-enabled negotiation, with explicit configurable thresholds for purchases below $25,000. He set savings expectations of 4 to 9 percent from autonomous negotiation, framing AI as the value driver rather than a feature on the wishlist.
• Amresh and ops lead Purav Desai treated AI as the core architecture. The integration requirement was that Zycus deliver an overlay solution on Oracle iProcurement, Oracle Fusion and ServiceNow without ripping iProcurement, with mandatory TSR review automation for sub-$25K critical software. That is buyer-led specification of where AI must plug in.
• Project sequencing reinforces the Hungry call: exploration through September, POC in December or January, go-live targeted May 2026 within roughly a year. Amresh is convening additional stakeholders including Chris and Alicia and power users. Two recorded sessions, one completed and one cancelled, plus action items committing both sides to AI-anchored next steps.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cornerstone went dark roughly 10.8 months after a strong 19 August 2025 demo; every buyer-stated milestone (September 2025 down-select, December 2025 or January 2026 POC, May 2026 go-live) passed with no confirmation, and every rep touch March to June 2026 drew no reply.
north_star_verdict.math: Created and qualified 25 July 2025. Last verified buyer engagement is the 19 August 2025 demo call, about 322 days (10.8 months) before this 6 July 2026 sweep. last_activity_date 26 June 2026 is rep-side only (Davis Thomson roundtable invites and Clari nudges, no buyer reply). Stage still Formal Evaluation (unchanged since the 14 April 2026 Shortlisted to Formal Evaluation downgrade); close 27 August 2026 is 52 days out. The buyer's own 19 August 2025 plan (explore to September 2025, down-select 1 to 2 vendors, POC December 2025 or January 2026, go-live May 2026) is fully lapsed with zero confirmed progress. Cold far beyond the buyer's stated cadence is a hard-kill signal at Formal Evaluation.
evidence: Last verified buyer engagement: 19 August 2025 demo call, about 10.8 months before this sweep

Every rep touch since drew no buyer reply: Clari emails to Amresh 9 Mar, 25 Mar, 7 Apr, 24 Apr, 22 Jun 2026; Davis Thomson roundtable invites to Purav, Justin and Chris 18-26 Jun 2026; outbound call to Alycia Stafford 4 May 2026 could not connect
Next Step 22 Jun 2026: 'Following up with Amresh to receive updated evaluation timeline, No response yet'; 29 Jun 2026 'No Material Update'
Buyer's planned end-September 2025 vendor down-select outcome was never shared
Planned December 2025 or January 2026 POC was never confirmed to have started
Planned May 2026 go-live is now past with no contract
Economic buyer (Amresh's 'head of global procurement to give his blessing', 19 Aug 2025) was never named or engaged
Chris Soriano (VP Strategic Sourcing), a stakeholder Amresh named on 19 Aug 2025, was never engaged; roundtable invite 25 Jun 2026 unanswered
Amount cut from $210k to $185k on 18 Mar 2026 and stage downgraded Shortlisted to Formal Evaluation on 14 Apr 2026, both internal-defensive moves
Close date 27 August 2026 (52 days) is indefensible for a Formal Evaluation deal dark for 10.8 months

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
metrics.narrative: On the 19 August 2025 demo Amresh Munshi set a concrete savings expectation for autonomous negotiation, saying 'assume that 4% is a savings target' with configured headroom to 6 to 9 percent, and he quantified the risk driver with a real incident: a roughly $10,000 add-on tool bought below threshold that bypassed the mandatory TSR review, which he called 'a security breach'. The value driver (AI-led tail-spend savings plus compliance control on sub-$25,000 spend) is buyer-articulated and partly numbered, but no validated ROI or total-savings model was ever built with Cornerstone, and the numbers are now about 10.8 months stale with no reconfirmation. Building a quantified savings and risk case with procurement ops lead Purav Desai is the reactivation move, not a claim we can bank today.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
economic_buyer.narrative: The true budget owner is unmapped. On 19 August 2025 Amresh Munshi (self-described project lead) said he would need 'my head of global procurement to be part of it to give his blessing and take it forward', but that person has never been named, met, or added to the relevant stakeholder most senior mapped contact is Chris Soriano, VP of Strategic Sourcing (OpportunityContactRole), who is a plausible decision-level sponsor but has never engaged (roundtable invite 25 June 2026 unanswered). No pricing or commercial-authority conversation has occurred with anyone, so budget authority is inferred from titles only. Naming and reaching the head of global procurement, using Chris Soriano as the lever, is a required gate move before this deal can advance.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
decision_criteria.narrative: Amresh Munshi laid out clear, deal-specific evaluation criteria on 19 August 2025: an AI-first overlay that sits on top of Oracle iProcurement and Oracle Fusion without replacing them, autonomous sourcing auto-triggered below a $25,000 (target) threshold, agentic negotiation that respects configured guardrails (Net 45 to Net 60 payment terms, 3 to 5 percent annual escalation ceiling), mandatory TSR-review routing for new software even below threshold, SSO via Okta/SAML 2.0, PII protection, and strategic-sourcing analytics for high-value SaaS and data-center categories. These are confirmed buyer-stated criteria, but they were captured in a single initial demo framed explicitly as a fit check ('whether we fit your scope or not'), and the formal weighting against GEP, iValua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba was never shared, so the criteria are documented but not scored.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
decision_process.narrative: Amresh described the buyer's intended process clearly on 19 August 2025: explore all providers through September 2025, run pricing and identification with the selected providers, down-select 1 to 2 vendors for a POC by December 2025 or January 2026, then partial contracting during the POC and final contracting within about a month of POC completion, targeting a May 2026 go-live (with a safer roughly one-year cushion). He positioned himself as project lead with Purav Desai coordinating scheduling, and committed to convene procurement ops, IT, power users, Chris Soriano and Alycia ('Alicia') Stafford, plus his head of global procurement for sign-off. The failure signal is that this documented process has not visibly moved a single step in 10.8 months: no down-select outcome, no POC, and the process owner has gone silent, so the real state is a stalled process, not an unclear one.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are only lightly sketched and now stale. On 19 August 2025 Amresh outlined a two-step commercial path (partial contracting to start the POC, then final contracting within about a month of POC completion), and flagged that ServiceNow is where Cornerstone runs legal review, contract management, IT security and privacy review, implying InfoSec and a TSR/security gate will apply. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, procurement channel, redline owner, or signatory has ever been discussed, and no contract-expiration forcing function exists (this is net-new, not a renewal). Because the deal never reached commercials, paper is a genuine unknown; visibility would only come from restarting the evaluation and reaching the procurement and legal owners in ServiceNow.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well established and owned by Amresh Munshi. Cornerstone has no source-to-pay system, so tail-spend supplier outreach happens 'outside the system, but via email' with no audit trail, creating Maverick-spend and process-compliance exposure. A real security incident already occurred: a roughly $10,000 sub-threshold software add-on was bought without the mandatory TSR review, which Amresh called 'a security breach' and tied to live PII and data-privacy concerns. He also framed Oracle as structural pain: iProcurement is locked into the Fusion ERP package on a 'take it or leave it' basis, and Oracle is 'behind in enhancing' agentic capability at 'very significantly high cost', a legacy of a 2021 decision to leave NetSuite and drop Coupa that he called a 'mistake'. This is confirmed operational and financial pain, not inferred, but it has not been re-voiced since August 2025.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
champion.narrative: Amresh Munshi was a strong emerging champion on 19 August 2025: 'this is really exciting, and we want to implement it', he praised Michael's leadership, asked to share Zycus collateral with his leaders, and committed to convene procurement ops, IT, power users, Chris Soriano, Alycia Stafford and his head of global procurement. But he has been unresponsive for about 10.8 months, ignoring Clari nudges (9 Mar, 25 Mar, 7 Apr, 24 Apr, 22 Jun 2026) and every roundtable invite, and he gate-kept every other thread, leaving Zycus single-threaded on one silent contact. Note he is named only in Salesforce free-text (AIS_Why__c, Next_Step__c) and outbound activity, not as an OpportunityContactRole, and carries no confirmed title beyond his self-described 'project lead'. What developed: genuine enthusiasm and detailed requirements; what is missing and now critical: any sustained engagement, an economic-buyer introduction, and a second internal advocate.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
competition.narrative: On 19 August 2025 Amresh said Cornerstone was 'exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement' and would 'onboard one or two of them to go for the POC', a parallel evaluation the Salesforce Competitors__c field records as GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba. Oracle iProcurement and Fusion is the entrenched incumbent that stays as the ERP and procure-to-pay baseline (Zycus overlays it, does not replace it), so the contest is for the agentic-AI source-to-pay overlay, not a rip-and-replace. After 10.8 months of silence and a lapsed September 2025 down-select, the single strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing, a stalled or deprioritized initiative, rather than any named rival, whose relative standing is unknown because the down-select outcome was never shared. Zycus's wedge if the deal reopens is the Merlin autonomous-sourcing and negotiation demo the buyer already praised, plus the Oracle-overlay fit and the Tata Sky and Tata Advanced Systems proof points.

deal_movement.summary: The only real buyer engagement remains the 19 August 2025 demo. Since then the record shows internal-defensive moves and unanswered rep outreach, not buyer progress: amount cut, stage downgraded, close date slipped twice, and Next_Step logged 'No Material Update'.

items.change: Amount cut from $210k to $185k by Michael McCarthy
Stage downgraded Shortlisted to Formal Evaluation, and forecast moved Upside Key Deal to Pipeline, by Michael McCarthy
Close date pushed 4 Jun to 27 Aug 2026 by Steve Ovadje after two earlier slips (25 Mar to 17 Apr, then to 4 Jun)
Division reclassified New Markets to Mid-Enterprise by Steve Ovadje
Next Step logged 'Following up with Amresh to receive updated evaluation timeline, No response yet', then 'No Material Update'

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing, a stalled or deprioritized initiative: the buyer's own September 2025 down-select, December 2025 or January 2026 POC and May 2026 go-live all passed with 10.8 months of silence, so the deal is at real risk of defaulting to no decision. The named field (GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) reflects the parallel evaluation Amresh described on 19 August 2025 but whose outcome was never shared, so those rivals sit at carried-forward medium threat pending fresh evidence, not re-ranked on this zero-new-call run. Oracle iProcurement and Fusion is the incumbent that stays as the ERP baseline (Zycus overlays it, does not replace it), a low current threat but the binding integration constraint. No new competitive evidence surfaced this run; threat levels are held.

competitors.name: Do-nothing (status quo / stalled initiative)
competitors.quote: 10.8-month silence since the 19 August 2025 demo; the buyer's planned September 2025 down-select, December 2025 or January 2026 POC and May 2026 go-live all lapsed with no confirmation and no reply to March-June 2026 outreach
competitors.how_we_win: Re-engage Amresh Munshi, or thread to Purav Desai and Chris Soriano, anchoring on the August 2025 pain (no source-to-pay, Maverick spend, the $10k TSR/PII breach) and the cost of continued inaction to make status quo untenable.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement, and the player who is having more capability... will onboard one or two of them to go for the POC
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin autonomous sourcing and agentic negotiation maturity plus MS Teams and Okta SSO and the Oracle/ServiceNow overlay fit; emphasize configurability and user experience versus GEP's heavier platform.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin agentic AI, autonomous negotiation, and strategic-sourcing analytics depth as the AI-first tail-spend overlay versus iValua's broader but less AI-mature suite.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin autonomous AI and intake simplicity plus the Oracle-overlay fit tailored to Cornerstone's Oracle Fusion and ServiceNow stack.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's AI-first architecture and overlay simplicity against Ariba's legacy complexity and cost; leverage Amresh's stated aversion to expensive large-suite lock-in (his 2021 Oracle Fusion 'mistake').

competitors.name: Oracle iProcurement / Fusion (incumbent)
competitors.quote: we cannot replace iProcurement... it is integrated with our Oracle ERP system, which is Oracle Fusion... Oracle has clearly said we are behind in enhancing that thing, and if we do it, it will be very significantly high cost, and we don't want to spend in Oracle right now
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
competitors.how_we_win: Overlay architecture: sit on top of Oracle iProcurement and Fusion without replacing them, delivering autonomous sourcing, Merlin intake, and TSR auto-flagging that Oracle cannot provide at acceptable cost.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: On 19 August 2025 Cornerstone's stated criteria aligned strongly with Zycus's overlay and agentic-AI story; the exposure is not fit but that the fit was never validated past a single demo and the buyer has since gone dark.

items.area: Whether the buyer's project is still active or deprioritized after 10.8 months of silence

best_practice_check.summary: This is a strong-fit deal that has gone cold on a single silent contact; winning it back is about restarting engagement through more than one door and making inaction visibly costly, not chasing new features.
flags: Break the single-thread: Amresh has been silent 10.8 months, so reach Purav Desai (named coordinator) and Chris Soriano (VP) directly, not only through Amresh.
Make status-quo untenable: re-anchor on the August 2025 $10k TSR/PII security breach and Maverick-spend exposure, the pain that justified the project.
Re-qualify the opportunity honestly: get the updated evaluation timeline in writing or move the forecast, because a 52-day close on a deal dark since August 2025 is not real.

champion_strength.summary: Weak and at high risk. Amresh Munshi advocated strongly on 19 August 2025 ('this is really exciting, and we want to implement it') and committed to convene the committee, but has been silent for about 10.8 months and non-responsive to every Clari nudge and roundtable invite March to June 2026 while gate-keeping all other threads. Enthusiasm developed; sustained engagement, an economic-buyer introduction, and a second advocate are missing.
champion_strength.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025; SF AIS_Why__c; SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026; SF activities Mar-Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic-AI story landed exceptionally well on 19 August 2025: Amresh treated autonomous sourcing and AI negotiation as the core architecture and specified exactly where AI must plug in (sub-$25k auto-sourcing, TSR auto-flagging, configured negotiation guardrails). The positioning is not the problem; the buyer going dark is. We are well-positioned on message, not under-positioned.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 8.0 / Strong, corroborated by the 19 August 2025 call where Amresh framed AI as the value driver, not a wishlist feature, and led the requirements on autonomous negotiation and agentic thresholds. The field read is accurate; the caveat is that this strong AI appetite has had no fresh confirmation in 10.8 months.

items.detail: Dark 10.8 months; every buyer-stated milestone (Sep 2025 down-select, Dec 2025/Jan 2026 POC, May 2026 go-live) lapsed and a 52-day close is indefensible.
Single-threaded on one silent contact (Amresh) who gate-keeps all others; no second advocate and no live relationship with Chris Soriano, Purav Desai, Justin Robinson or Alycia Stafford.
items.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025; SF activities Mar-Jun 2026

items.detail: Internal-defensive signals: amount cut $210k to $185k (18 Mar 2026) and stage/forecast downgraded (14 Apr 2026), consistent with a cooling or descoping deal.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cold. The last two-way buyer exchange was the 19 August 2025 demo; everything since is one-directional rep outreach with no reply, and the Next Step reads 'No Material Update'.
items: Zero buyer replies to five Clari nudges and multiple roundtable invites Mar-Jun 2026
Buyer's own down-select, POC and go-live dates all passed silently
Amount cut and stage downgraded internally

scope_change.to: AI overlay (Merlin intake plus autonomous sourcing on top of Oracle iProcurement/Fusion) scoped at $185,000
scope_change.detail: Amount was cut $210k to $185k on 18 Mar 2026 and the deal is framed as an overlay, not a procure-to-pay replacement ('we cannot replace iProcurement... what we are looking for is source to pay as well as agentic AI on top of it'). Narrowing scope plus a lapsed timeline reads as the buyer getting defensive and deprioritizing, reinforcing the do-nothing risk.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A textbook-strong AI fit that has gone cold: Amresh Munshi loved the 19 August 2025 demo and hand-drew the requirements, but he has been dark 10.8 months, the whole timeline he set has lapsed, and the one thing between us and a real deal is simply getting any buyer to respond and re-confirm the evaluation is alive.

win_position.text: Position is weak: the deal is dark 10.8 months since the 19 August 2025 demo, single-threaded on a silent Amresh Munshi, with no economic buyer engaged and the amount already cut $210k to $185k.
The upside is real fit: on 19 August 2025 Amresh said 'this is really exciting, and we want to implement it' and specified exactly where autonomous AI must plug in, and AIS reads 8.0 Strong.

Every buyer-stated milestone lapsed (Sep 2025 down-select, Dec 2025/Jan 2026 POC, May 2026 go-live) and five Clari nudges plus June roundtable invites drew no reply, so status-quo/no-decision is now the front-runner over any named rival.
The competitive down-select against GEP, iValua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba was never reported back, so we do not even know if we made the shortlist.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is negative: no buyer touch since 19 August 2025, and the only recent record moves are internal (amount cut 18 Mar, stage downgraded Shortlisted to Formal Evaluation 14 Apr, close pushed to 27 Aug 2026).
Next Step on 22 Jun 2026 reads 'Following up with Amresh to receive updated evaluation timeline, No response yet' and 29 Jun 2026 'No Material Update', confirming the stall.

customer_commitment.text: No current commitment: the buyer owes us an updated evaluation timeline (asked 22 Jun 2026) and has not responded, and no commercial or POC step was ever confirmed.
Historically Amresh committed to convene procurement ops, IT, power users and his head of global procurement, but that was 19 August 2025 and none of it materialized.

deal_risk.text: Highest risk is no-decision: Cornerstone keeps limping along on Oracle iProcurement plus email-based tail-spend and may simply never sign, a stalled-initiative loss rather than a loss to GEP or Ariba.
Single-thread and unmapped economic buyer mean one silent contact can freeze the entire deal, exactly what has happened.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer engagement in 10.8 months; only internal amount/stage/date moves; Next Step 'No Material Update' 29 Jun 2026.

customer_commitment.evidence: Buyer non-responsive to five Clari nudges and June roundtable invites; owes an updated timeline since 22 Jun 2026.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing is the strongest current threat; single-threaded on a silent champion with no EB mapped.

customer_preference.evidence: Amresh was highly positive on 19 August 2025 ('really impressed', 'we want to implement it'), but that enthusiasm is 10.8 months stale with no reconfirmation and no reply to any 2026 outreach, so there is no active current preference; the deal is drifting toward no-decision.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain plus a buyer-stated 4 to 9 percent autonomous-negotiation savings target and a concrete $10k TSR/PII breach example, but no validated ROI or total-savings model was built (19 August 2025).

items.action: Steve emails Amresh a fresh Merlin agentic-sourcing proof point tied to his LinkedIn AI posts.
items.trigger: Clari nudge 22 Jun 2026 noted 'keeping up with your AI in procurement posts on LinkedIn'; no reply
items.expected_effect: Reopens the champion thread with a value hook rather than another status chase, the prerequisite to any progress.

items.action: Reach Purav Desai directly for the current evaluation status and vendor shortlist outcome.
items.trigger: Purav was named coordinator on 19 Aug 2025; roundtable invite 22 Jun 2026 unanswered
items.expected_effect: Breaks the single-thread on Amresh and gets a truthful read on whether the project is alive or deprioritized.

items.action: Open a second thread to Chris Soriano anchored on the $10k TSR/PII breach and Maverick-spend risk.
items.trigger: Chris Soriano (VP Strategic Sourcing) never engaged; roundtable invite 25 Jun 2026 unanswered
items.expected_effect: Reaches decision-level power and makes inaction costly, moving toward the unmapped economic buyer.

items.action: If no buyer reply by 22 Jul, re-qualify the deal and correct the forecast and close date.
items.trigger: Deal dark since 19 Aug 2025; 52-day close on Formal Evaluation is indefensible
items.expected_effect: Keeps the pipeline honest and frees effort if Cornerstone has genuinely gone to no-decision.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount was cut $210k to $185k on 18 Mar 2026 and the deal is framed as an overlay, not a procure-to-pay replacement ('we cannot replace iProcurement... what we  (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 14 is carried by business case strength +0.30 (weight 10); momentum 52 vs stage-expected 52. Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount was cut $210k to $185k on 18 Mar 2026 and the deal is framed as an overlay, not a procure-to-pay….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (14), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
